kept.text:
CopilotKit (Techstars 2023), a startup building the open standard for how AI agents communicate with software, has raised $27M. 

Founded by brothers Atai Barkai and Uli Barkai, CopilotKit created AG-UI (Agent-User Interaction) a protocol that lets AI agents generate interactive charts, update dashboards, and take actions inside existing applications. 🤖 Companies including Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Oracle have adopted the protocol, and more than half of the Fortune 500 use its tools.

CopilotKit's core technology is open source, with more than 40,000 GitHub stars and millions of installs per week. The $27M raise, comprising a $20M Series A and a $7M seed round, was led by Glilot Capital, NFX, and SignalFire. 

Huge congratulations to the entire CopilotKit team on this milestone!

https://t.co/zVMOipnlBW

profile_bio.description: Helping founders scale their startups into world-changing businesses.

kept.text:
I first took Andrew Ng's Machine Learning course on "iTunes U" in 2008 BC (Before Coursera)

20 years later, I got to make a new course alongside him

Introducing: "Interactive Agents with Generative UI"

In 2 hours, you'll learn how to build a Claude-like fullstack agentic app that renders interactive UI on the fly.

All UI will be AI

profile_bio.description: CEO @CopilotKit🪁 | ex-@Meta | Author of PsyPhy (experimentally testable physics theory of consciousness) |  Physics BA & MSc @Penn

quoted_tweet.text:
New course: Build agents that respond to users with not only plaintext, but custom UIs like charts, forms, and whiteboards, generated on demand and displayed right in the chat. This short course is built in partnership with  @CopilotKit and taught by @ataiiam, co-founder of CopilotKit.

You'll learn three approaches: Your agent can pick from custom components you build, like charts and forms. It can compose new layouts from a set of building blocks you provide, like rows, cards, and text. Or it can incorporate existing third-party apps, like a whiteboard or a calendar, right inside the conversation.

Skills you’ll gain:
- Build agents that render custom components like charts and forms on demand
- Build an app where the agent and user collaborate on shared data, beyond just the chat window
- Place third-party apps like maps, calendars, and whiteboards right in your interface

Join and build agents that give users something to see and act on! https://t.co/lvMy0YdF3z

profile_bio.description: Co-Founder of Coursera; Stanford CS adjunct faculty. Former head of Baidu AI Group/Google Brain. #ai #machinelearning, #deeplearning #MOOCs

kept.text:
All UI will be AI

It was so great to collaborate with Andrew Ng, Summer and the @DeepLearningAI team on this course 🎉

Really proud of the final product and I think it's an important missing piece of educational content in the ecosystem.

Also shoutout to @tyler_cpk , @blovedev and @ulidabess who helped bring this together on the  @CopilotKit side!

I'll be sharing more thoughts on this soon, but check out our course on Interactive Agents and Generative UI 👇

kept.text:
@ShroomSleep Always come away from an AlphaNooner with Christian with some great insight on how the psychedelic medicine space is developing and of course $ATAI. 
Looking forward to seeing you there, @ShroomSleep! Bring any questions if you have them.

Why we made this course 👇

Despite all the progress made in the agent ecosystem over the last few years, the UX layer of facilitating Rich Interactive Agents is still wholeheartedly underutilized.

Most companies are still shipping agents that either just live in the terminal or live as isolated 'chat boxes' in your app, that inadute users with long 'walls of text'

Two of the reasons for this gap are capabilities and education.

We are working to close both of these gaps - and partnered with the best of the best on the latter.

In this course we walk developers through:

1. Building an interactive Chat with Generative UI
2. Connecting your application with any agentic backend using AG-UI
3. Understanding the full specterum of Generative UI and how to build with it
4. Building rich fullstack agentic applications with shared state, frontend tool calls and human in the loop

and more!

We put a lot of heart into this course and I am really proud of the result. It's genuinely dense full of high quality education that you will not be able to find anywhere else.

tweet.text:
Why $CMPS, $DFTX & $ATAI Are Positioned for Significant Valuation Expansion

Wall Street analysts are increasingly highlighting blockbuster revenue potential for the leading psychedelic developers as late-stage catalysts build and regulatory momentum intensifies.

For $DFTX, Piper Sandler and others see DT120 (optimized LSD ODT — orally disintegrating tablet) targeting peak annual U.S. sales north of $3B in GAD (generalized anxiety disorder) and MDD (major depressive disorder). This is backed by Spravato-style pricing and rapid/durable Phase 2 signals from one of the most studied compounds in psychiatric history. Positive Phase 3 readouts in similar assets have often triggered 4–10x valuation gains or attractive M&A deals. At today’s ~$2.55B market cap, that rerating math points to $10B–$25B+ valuations — translating to roughly $90–$230 per share potential.

$CMPS stands out with its COMP360 synthetic psilocybin program, delivering strong Phase 3 results across large treatment-resistant depression (TRD) cohorts, now under rolling FDA review with a Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher in hand. Analysts project peak annual sales potential of ~$1.5–2.3B for COMP360 in TRD (with expansion upside into MDD and beyond), as a single-session therapy with multi-month durability eyes a meaningful share of the massive TRD market. At today’s ~$1.3B market cap, positive Phase 3 de-risking and approval could support 4–8x rerating math to $5–10B+ valuations — translating to roughly $37–$74 per share potential (Street consensus sits in the $20–25 range, with bulls to $60–70+).

$ATAI brings a broad portfolio strategy, headlined by BPL-003 intranasal DMT showing robust Phase 2b data in TRD with Phase 3 preparations underway. Analysts see peak annual sales potential of $3–5B+ for BPL-003 in TRD alone (Spravato-style interventional model), offering strong upside across TRD, substance use disorders, and additional indications if its lead assets deliver. At today’s ~$1.53B market cap, clean late-stage success could drive 4–8x valuation expansion to $6–12B+ — pointing to roughly $16–$33 per share potential (average Street targets land in the $14–18 zone, highs to $25).

Look at historical biotech comps: clean Phase 3 wins in mental health and neurology have consistently delivered multi-billion market caps and sharp rerates once de-risked. The clinical data keeps getting stronger. And the path to approval and commercialization is accelerating faster than most investors realize.

Well-positioned investors are lined up for a major move higher as these programs advance through 2026.
